_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 Alaska House incumbents (Anchorage-area HD19-23). 4 progressive Dems + 1 conservative R. Every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no public position found; never inferred from party.

genevieve-mina.notes_append: RESOLUTE evidence pass 2026-06-17: Anchorage HD-19 Democrat, sitting incumbent (term ends Jan 2027), up in 2026. Pro-abortion ('Abortion is health care, govt should not control access'; supports Medicaid abortion funding), endorsed by Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates. Sponsor of HB195 (pharmacist prescribing) opposed by Alaska Right to Life. Co-sponsor of HB89, Alaska's red-flag/GVPO bill (named in NRA-ILA opposition alert). No documented positions on marriage, religious liberty, federalism, immigration, or policing. Zero conservative positions found.

0.note: Stated abortion is health care and govt should not control access; cites AK privacy clause — contrary to affirming personhood at conception

1.note: Sponsored HB195 opposed by Alaska Right to Life as expanding chemical-abortion access; advocates abortion access, opposite of abolition

2.note: No public position found on embryonic research/IVF/chimeric

3.note: No public position found on euthanasia/assisted suicide

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates (abortion-industry political arm)

0.note: No public position found

andrew-gray.notes_append: RESOLUTE evidence pass 2026-06-17: Anchorage HD-20 Democrat, sitting House Judiciary chair, incumbent running 2026 (term ends Jan 2027). One of Alaska's first openly gay legislators (married to a man). Pro-abortion ('protect access to safe and legal abortion'), PP-aligned. Led failed pro-trans amendments against the trans-sports ban; champions Florida-style red-flag law and co-introduced gun-storage mandate HB164. Zero conservative positions found.

0.note: Says Alaska should continue protecting access to safe and legal abortion — contrary to affirming personhood at conception

1.note: Pro-abortion-access, welcomed by Planned Parenthood as elected leader; opposite of abolition

4.note: Endorsed/backed by Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates (abortion-industry political arm)

0.note: Openly gay, married to a man — does not affirm marriage as one man/one woman

1.note: In a same-sex marriage; does not oppose same-sex marriage

2.note: Introduced amendment affirming transgender constitutional rights and led filibuster against the trans-athletes restriction — affirms transgender ideology

3.note: No position found on no-fault divorce

4.note: Actively promoted LGBTQ protections in policy; opposed limits on transgender participation

0.note: No position found on school choice/homeschool

1.note: Opposes GOP parental-rights framing per news characterization, but no direct vote on parental consent for gender care; left null

3.note: Sponsored a social-media-harms-to-minors bill; not porn age-verification — null

donna-mears.notes_append: RESOLUTE evidence pass 2026-06-17: East Anchorage HD-21 Democrat, sitting incumbent, confirmed running for reelection Nov 3, 2026. Pro-abortion ('private matter...entirely up to the person seeking an abortion'), PP 'Repro Champ' endorsee; co-sponsored HB99 LGBTQ2S+ nondiscrimination. Wants permanent mail-in opt-in / eliminate witness signature. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: documented pro-police — 'fully supports providing APD and the Troopers with the tools they need to fully staff,' endorsed by the Anchorage Police Department Employee Association.

0.note: iVoterGuide questionnaire: abortion is 'a private matter, and entirely up to the person seeking an abortion' — contrary to affirming personhood at conception

4.note: Endorsed as a Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates 'Repro Champ' (abortion-industry political arm)

ted-eischeid.notes_append: RESOLUTE evidence pass 2026-06-17: Anchorage HD-22 Democrat, freshman (seated Jan 2025, flipped seat from R incumbent), running 2026. Former science teacher. Pro-abortion ('Protect women's right to choose'; 'robust sex education'), co-sponsored HB69 education-funding/BSA increase, pro-union; police/fire union endorsements + public-safety-funding support. Thin culture-war record — most social/constitutional areas returned no documented position.

0.note: Campaign pledges to 'Protect women's right to choose'; calls abortion part of reproductive health care — contrary to affirming personhood at conception

1.note: Pro-choice; opposite of abortion abolition

jamie-allard.notes_append: RESOLUTE evidence pass 2026-06-17: Eagle River/Chugiak HD-23 Republican, sitting incumbent (won 2024 general 61.8%), no evidence of withdrawal/other office. Hardline social conservative: prime sponsor HB183 (trans-sports ban by birth sex) and HB338 (extended liability for minor gender-affirming care); self-described pro-life with floor amendment to bar abortion-inducing drugs; 'Faith is my compass' + Religious Freedom Day op-ed on mandates; pro-APD (sought alternate body-cam funding); signed RCV-repeal op-ed; full statutory PFD support; Alaska Family Council endorsed (iVoterGuide rates 'Conservative'). No documented deviations from the conservative position.

0.note: Self-describes 'Pro-Life' on official bio; offered floor amendment to bar pharmacists from prescribing abortion-inducing drugs

1.note: Documented restrictions (anti-chemical-abortion amendment) but no abortion-abolition advocacy — abolition is a higher bar, left null

2.note: No position found on embryonic research/IVF

4.note: Pro-life Republican endorsed by Alaska Family Council; no abortion-industry funding

0.note: No direct statement on marriage as one man/one woman found

1.note: No position found on same-sex marriage in law

2.note: Prime sponsor HB183 (sports by sex assigned at birth) and HB338 (liability for minor gender-affirming care) — affirms biological sex, rejects transgender ideology

3.note: No position found on no-fault divorce

4.note: Sponsored HB183 restricting gender-ideology in school/college sports — opposes LGBTQ promotion in schools
